food_name: Deluxe Jolliburger Meal with Double Patties
food_subgroup: Fast food burger meal with sides

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic hamburger class (FOODON_00002383). No specific entry exists for a double-patty deluxe burger meal set. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Hamburger) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a double-patty fast food burger meal set.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for a double-patty fast food burger meal. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual fast food menu items at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One full meal set consisting of a double-patty deluxe burger (approximately 250 g), a regular serving of french fries (approximately 120 g), and a regular soft drink (approximately 280 ml), totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: Hamburger, double patty with condiments and vegetables — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21110)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for a double-patty hamburger with condiments and vegetables was used as the primary nutrient anchor for the burger component. French fries nutrient data adapted from USDA SR Legacy (fast food french fries, NDB 21138). Composite meal values were weighted by estimated component mass fractions (burger ~250 g, fries ~120 g, drink ~280 g water-equivalent with negligible nutrients). The composite per-100 g values represent the solid food components (burger + fries, ~370 g combined) as the nutritionally significant fraction. Soft drink contribution to macros is minimal and noted separately. Values represent a reasonable estimation for this meal type sold at Jollibee Singapore.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fast food meals — hamburger and french fries composite; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Meal with Double Patties. GI of 66 is estimated from the International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) using the closest analogous fast food meal: hamburger bun (GI ~61) and french fries (GI ~63–75, mean ~70). A weighted composite GI was calculated based on carbohydrate contribution: burger bun carbohydrate ~35 g (GI 61) and fries carbohydrate ~38 g (GI 70), yielding a weighted mean GI of approximately 66. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (66 × 68.8) / 100 ≈ 45.4, where carbohydrate per serving is estimated at 68.8 g (26.5 g per 100 g × 260 g solid food serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; estimated ~1,248 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal, as a single serving may contribute more than 50% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2,000 mg, significantly elevating blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic load (~45 GL per serving) and refined carbohydrate content from burger bun and french fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal due to its high glycemic load and refined carbohydrate content, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion reduction and substitution of fries with a lower-GI side are recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.3 g per 100 g), trans fat (~0.3 g per 100 g), sodium, and cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this meal due to its combined saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium load, all of which are independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk; occasional consumption only is advised within a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Deluxe Jolliburger Meal with Double Patties is a fast food burger meal set sold by Jollibee, a Filipino-origin fast food chain with outlets in Singapore including Bugis Village. The meal consists of a deluxe-style burger featuring two seasoned beef patties stacked in a soft sesame-seed bun, garnished with fresh lettuce, tomato slices, cheese, and mayonnaise-based sauce, served alongside a regular portion of crispy french fries and a regular soft drink. The burger patties are grilled or flame-cooked and seasoned, delivering a savoury, slightly smoky flavour with a juicy texture. The bun is soft and slightly sweet, characteristic of Jollibee's house style. The meal is certified Halal, making it accessible to Muslim consumers in Singapore. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense, high-sodium, moderate-to-high fat meal with a significant glycemic load, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The double-patty configuration increases protein content relative to a single-patty version, providing approximately 11 g of protein per 100 g of the solid food components. The meal is ultra-processed and is best consumed occasionally within a balanced dietary pattern. It is priced at SGD 11.50 at Jollibee Bugis Village as listed on field survey data.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Jollibee double burger meal?
Is the Jollibee Jolliburger halal in Singapore?
Jollibee double patty burger nutrition facts

Fast food burger meal health impact Singapore

confidence_reason: No direct nutrient data exists for the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Meal with Double Patties in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values were estimated by adapting USDA SR Legacy data for a double-patty hamburger with condiments and vegetables (NDB 21110) and fast food french fries (NDB 21138), weighted by estimated component mass fractions. GI was estimated from the International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al., 2008) using analogous fast food components. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entries (double-patty hamburger with condiments and vegetables, and fast food french fries) weighted by estimated component mass fractions. Direct laboratory analysis of the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Meal with Double Patties was not available in any approved source at time of processing. 2. Serving size of 650 g is an estimate: burger ~250 g, fries ~120 g, soft drink ~280 ml. The per-100 g values represent the solid food components (burger + fries, ~370 g) as the nutritionally significant fraction; the soft drink contributes primarily water and sugar and is noted separately. 3. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on weighted carbohydrate contributions from burger bun (GI ~61) and french fries (GI ~70) per Atkinson et al. (2008), Diabetes Care. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation. 7. The Halal certification is based on the cuisine_tags provided in the input seed record (Jollibee Singapore is a Halal-certified chain); this should be verified against the official Halal certification body (MUIS Singapore) for production use. 8. Price of SGD 11.50 is preserved from the field survey data seed record and reflects the price at Jollibee Bugis Village at time of data collection.
